quotes: H1 (section hero headline): 28px / 36px (≈130%), Nunito Sans ExtraBold or Bold
H2 (section heading): 22px / 30px (≈136%), Nunito Sans Bold
H3 (subhead): 18px / 26px (≈144%), Nunito Sans Bold or SemiBold
Secondary/supporting body: 14px / 21px (150%), Nunito Sans Regular

relations.note: Minimum body/running-text sizes constrain the email type scale.
Line-height minimums apply to the email type scale sizes.
Weight choices constrained by the assigned font family per medium.

quotes: Headlines & headers | **Agenda** (Adobe font) | **Nunito Sans** (Google font), bolder weights, Title Case; *Agenda may be used as live text in digital for the campaign headline only*
Body copy | **Nunito Sans** | **Nunito Sans** | **Aptos**
Email font stack (CSS):** `'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif`
Headlines & headers | **Agenda** (Adobe font) | **Nunito Sans** (Google font), bolder weights, Title Case; *Agenda may be used as live text in digital for the campaign headline only*
Body copy | **Nunito Sans** | **Nunito Sans** | **Aptos**
Footers, fine print | Nunito Sans | Nunito Sans | Aptos
Nunito Sans must be loaded via `@font-face`/Google Fonts link where the client supports it; the stack guarantees graceful fallback in Outlook and other clients that strip webfonts.

rules.intent: Enforce consistent brand fonts across all production media with a reliable email fallback.
rules.rule_text: Font families are assigned by medium: headlines/headers use Agenda in print (headline.font = Agenda) and Nunito Sans (Title Case, bolder weights) in digital/email; body copy, footers and fine print use Nunito Sans (body.font = Nunito Sans); Word/PPT/Excel fallback is Aptos. Agenda may be used as live text in digital for the campaign headline only (campaign_headline.font = Agenda). The email CSS font stack is 'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif, with Nunito Sans loaded via @font-face/Google Fonts where supported to guarantee graceful fallback in Outlook and clients that strip webfonts.

quotes: Available Agenda weights: Light, Regular, Medium, Semibold, Bold.
Available Nunito Sans weights: Light, Regular, SemiBold, Bold, ExtraBold.
Available Aptos weights: Regular, Bold.

rules.intent: Restrict typographic weights to the approved set per font family.
rules.rule_text: Only the sanctioned weights per family may be used: Agenda in Light, Regular, Medium, Semibold, Bold; Nunito Sans in Light, Regular, SemiBold, Bold, ExtraBold; Aptos in Regular, Bold.

quotes: H1 (section hero headline): 28px / 36px (≈130%), Nunito Sans ExtraBold or Bold
H2 (section heading): 22px / 30px (≈136%), Nunito Sans Bold
H3 (subhead): 18px / 26px (≈144%), Nunito Sans Bold or SemiBold
Body: 16px / 24px (150%), Nunito Sans Regular
Secondary/supporting body: 14px / 21px (150%), Nunito Sans Regular — use sparingly, never for primary content
Footnote/reference text within a section: 12px / 18px (150%)
**[GENERAL] Email type scale (px / line-height):**
H1 (section hero headline): 28px / 36px (≈130%), Nunito Sans ExtraBold or Bold
Footnote/reference text within a section: 12px / 18px (150%) — must be "noticeably smaller" than primary data and clearly separated from it

rules.intent: Define a consistent, accessible email type scale.
rules.rule_text: The [GENERAL] email type scale (px / line-height): H1 section hero headline 28px/36px (≈130%) Nunito Sans ExtraBold or Bold; H2 section heading 22px/30px (≈136%) Nunito Sans Bold; H3 subhead 18px/26px (≈144%) Nunito Sans Bold or SemiBold; Body 16px/24px (150%) Nunito Sans Regular; Secondary/supporting body 14px/21px (150%) Nunito Sans Regular (use sparingly, never for primary content); Footnote/reference text within a section 12px/18px (150%), which must be noticeably smaller than primary data and clearly separated from it. Note: H1 body size (28px) and H2 body size (22px) have no matching size tokens in catalog.

quotes: 16px is the brand-mandated minimum for body copy ("bigger is better")
Never set running text below 12px.
**16px is the brand-mandated minimum for body copy ("bigger is better")**
Never set running text below 12px. (ISI sizing is owned by the locked component.)

rules.intent: Guarantee legible minimum text sizes for accessibility.
rules.rule_text: 16px is the brand-mandated minimum for body copy ("bigger is better") (size.body.min = 16px). Never set running text below 12px (size.min.running_text = 12px). ISI sizing is owned by the locked component, not this rule.

quotes: Line height: minimum 150% for all body copy
For light text on dark backgrounds, increase further** — [GENERAL] use 160% (e.g., 16px/26px).
**Line height: minimum 150% for all body copy** (brand accessibility rule). **For light text on dark backgrounds, increase further** — [GENERAL] use 160% (e.g., 16px/26px).

rules.intent: Ensure readable line spacing, with extra spacing for light-on-dark text.
rules.rule_text: Body line height is a minimum of 150% for all body copy (body.line_height = 150%), a brand accessibility rule. For light text on dark backgrounds, increase further — use 160% (line_height.light_on_dark = 160%, e.g. 16px/26px).

effect.subject: two adjacent text blocks at same size+weight

quotes: Never use two adjacent text blocks at the same size+weight to signal different hierarchy levels.
Use a clear **type scale / visual hierarchy** so the reader knows what to read first, next, last. Never use two adjacent text blocks at the same size+weight to signal different hierarchy levels.

rules.intent: Preserve legible reading order through distinct hierarchy levels.
rules.rule_text: Use a clear type scale / visual hierarchy so the reader knows what to read first, next, last. Never use two adjacent text blocks at the same size+weight to signal different hierarchy levels.
